items.title: Lima Township Residents Block Proposed 1.4-Gigawatt Power Plant Amid Saline Data Center Concerns
items.dek: A rural Washtenaw County community successfully halted plans for a massive natural gas plant that would have supplied power amid questions over connections to the nearby Oracle-OpenAI data center project.
items.judge_note: Concrete outcome: residents successfully blocked a 1.4-gigawatt power plant with clear connection to Oracle-OpenAI data center project.

items.title: Adrian enacts year-long pause on new data center proposals
items.dek: City leaders approved a moratorium to study impacts before allowing more projects. The move gives residents time to weigh power demand, water use, and noise concerns. Other Michigan communities are likely to watch Adrian's review closely.
items.judge_note: Clear municipal action: Adrian approved a year-long moratorium, a definitive policy decision affecting future data center development.

items.title: Saline Township weighs 12-year tax break for Oracle's $43 billion project
items.dek: The board will decide whether to grant major tax relief for one of the largest data center investments in state history. The outcome affects local school funding and services for years. Voters and businesses are watching how the deal balances jobs against tax revenue.
items.judge_note: Major pending decision on $43 billion project tax break with significant fiscal implications for schools and services.

items.title: Girard Township OKs data center rules as Coldwater starts drafting its own
items.dek: Two southwest Michigan townships move forward on zoning and permitting standards for data centers. The actions signal how local governments are responding to rapid industry interest. Residents will see clearer rules on where and how these projects can proceed.
items.judge_note: Two townships advancing specific zoning rules represents concrete regulatory development, though incremental.
